title: Indagine urbinate: "Così l'algoritmo influenza il voto"
snippet: This news article discusses a study by Fabio Giglietto, a sociologist at the University of Urbino Carlo Bo, on how social media algorithms can influence elections and democracy. The article quotes Giglietto and highlights the study's findings on Meta's political content reduction policy and its asymmetrical effect on elected representatives versus extremist accounts.
description: This news article reports on a study by Fabio Giglietto of the University of Urbino Carlo Bo, detailing his research on how social media algorithms influence elections and the impact of Meta's content policies.

title: Indagine urbinate: "Così l’algoritmo influenza il voto" - Il Resto del Carlino

title: There's Research on That - The Society Pages
snippet: This article from The Society Pages, dated February 25, 2026, cites a 2019 paper co-authored by Fabio Giglietto, "'Fake news' is the invention of a liar: How false information circulates within the hybrid news system," in a broader discussion about anti-intellectualism and the circulation of false information.
description: An article from The Society Pages, dated February 25, 2026, discusses anti-intellectualism and the circulation of false information, citing Fabio Giglietto's 2019 research paper on 'fake news' circulation.

title: Facebook, troppa visibilità a estremisti e complottisti: ecco come impatta Meta sulle elezioni
snippet: The article reports on a research study from the University of Urbino, authored by Professor Fabio Giglietto, which found that Facebook's algorithm penalizes institutional political visibility while promoting extremist and conspiratorial content. The study analyzed over 2.5 million posts.
description: The Lumsanews article reports on a research study led by Professor Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino, analyzing Facebook's algorithmic promotion of extremist content and its impact on elections.
